401.reason: [When an expected terminal is missing, the parser should still advance the token stream to avoid infinite loops or repeated error reporting, but currently it just returns without consuming any token.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [When an expected terminal is missing, the parser should still advance the token stream to avoid infinite loops or getting stuck, but this code returns without consuming any token.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [When an expected terminal is missing, the parser should still advance the token stream to avoid infinite loops or repeated error reporting, but currently it just returns without consuming any token.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [When a syntax error is detected (e.g., missing semicolon, parenthesis, or bracket), the method returns without consuming any token. This causes the parser to remain stuck on the same token, potentially leading to infinite loops or cascading errors because the parser never advances past the problematic position.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [When a required terminal is missing, the method returns without consuming any token, causing the parser to remain on the same token indefinitely and potentially leading to infinite loops or incorrect error recovery. The parser should still advance the token stream even when an error is detected to avoid getting stuck.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [When an expected terminal is missing, the parser should still consume a token to avoid infinite loops, but this code returns without advancing the token stream, causing potential infinite loops or repeated error reporting.]

978.reason: [For error types like missing semicolon or parenthesis, the error line should correspond to the line of the last successfully parsed token before the expected terminal, but using index-1 may not always point to the correct syntactic component's last token, especially after backtracking or in complex nested structures.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [For error types like missing semicolon or parenthesis, the error line should correspond to the line of the last successfully parsed token before the expected terminal, but using index-1 may not always point to the correct syntactic component's last token, especially after backtracking or in complex nested structures.]
